samples.gvd:
module pulse_detect #(
    parameter s0 = 2'b00,
    parameter s1 = 2'b01,
    parameter s2 = 2'b10,
    parameter s3 = 2'b11
) (
    input clk,
    input rst_n,
    input data_in,
    output reg data_out
);

parameter INITIAL = 2'b00;
parameter PULSE_START = 2'b01;
parameter PULSE_MIDDLE = 2'b10;
parameter PULSE_END = 2'b11;

reg [1:0] state;

always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
    if (~rst_n) begin
        state <= INITIAL;
    end else begin
        case (state)
            INITIAL: begin
                if (data_in == 1'b1) begin
                    state <= PULSE_START;
                end else begin
                    state <= INITIAL;
                end
            end
            PULSE_START: begin
                if (data_in == 1'b0) begin
                    state <= PULSE_MIDDLE;
                end else begin
                    state <= PULSE_START;
                end
            end
            PULSE_MIDDLE: begin
                if (data_in == 1'b1) begin
                    state <= PULSE_END;
                end else begin
                    state <= INITIAL;
                end
            end
            PULSE_END: begin
                if (data_in == 1'b0) begin
                    state <= INITIAL;
                end else begin
                    state <= PULSE_START;
                end
            end
        endcase
    end
end

always @(*) begin
    case (state)
        PULSE_END: begin
            if (data_in == 1'b0) begin
                data_out = 1'b1;
            end else begin
                data_out = 1'b0;
            end
        end
        default: begin
            data_out = 1'b0;
        end
    endcase
end
endmodule
samples.xml_description:
<module id="pulse_detect">
    <description>Implements pulse detection logic based on the input signal data_in.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">The clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">The active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="data_in">One-bit input signal.</input>
        <output id="data_out">Output signal indicating the presence of pulses.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <state id="INITIAL">Initial state of the pulse detection process.</state>
        <state id="PULSE_START">State representing the start of a pulse.</state>
        <state id="PULSE_MIDDLE">State representing the middle of a pulse.</state>
        <state id="PULSE_END">State representing the end of a pulse.</state>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="state">Register to store the current state of the pulse detection process.</reg>
        <always_comb>
            case(state)
                INITIAL: begin
                    if(data_in == 1'b1) begin
                        state <= PULSE_START;
                    end else begin
                        state <= INITIAL;
                    end
                end
                PULSE_START: begin
                    if(data_in == 1'b0) begin
                        state <= PULSE_MIDDLE;
                    end else begin
                        state <= PULSE_START;
                    end
                end
                PULSE_MIDDLE: begin
                    if(data_in == 1'b1) begin
                        state <= PULSE_END;
                    end else begin
                        state <= INITIAL;
                    end
                end
                PULSE_END: begin
                    if(data_in == 1'b0) begin
                        state <= INITIAL;
                    end else begin
                        state <= PULSE_START;
                    end
                end
            endcase
        </always_comb>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        // Implementing the pulse detection logic based on the provided state machine
        reg [1:0] state_reg;
        
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if(~rst_n) begin
                state_reg <= 2'b00;
            end else begin
                case(state_reg)
                    2'b00: begin
                        if(data_in == 1'b1) begin
                            state_reg <= 2'b01;
                        end else begin
                            state_reg <= 2'b00;
                        end
                    end
                    2'b01: begin
                        if(data_in == 1'b0) begin
                            state_reg <= 2'b10;
                        end else begin
                            state_reg <= 2'b01;
                        end
                    end
                    2'b10: begin
                        if(data_in == 1'b1) begin
                            state_reg <= 2'b11;
                        end else begin
                            state_reg <= 2'b00;
                        end
                    end
                    2'b11: begin
                        if(data_in == 1'b0) begin
                            state_reg <= 2'b00;
                        end else begin
                            state_reg <= 2'b01;
                        end
                    end
                endcase
            end
        end
        
        assign data_out = (state_reg == 2'b11) ? 1'b1 : 1'b0;
    </implementation>
</module>
